title: Glasswater
premise: A sun-bleached desert where the old canals have fused to glass in the heat, and water is not a resource but the law itself.

regions.name: the Glass Canal
regions.blurb: The spine of the country: the old canal, fused to glass, and every cart and toll-house on it.

arrive: she came down onto the canal road at {place}. there is always someone on it, and everyone on it is going somewhere they have to be.
into {region} out of {from}. the glass runs straight to the horizon here, and she can be seen for a mile in either direction, and so can everyone else.
she is on the canal again. {place} by nightfall, and the glass ringing under cartwheels all night, which she had forgotten and did not miss.
{region}: tolls, traffic, and {agents} at every crossing. she has coin, so it is only expensive, and not yet a problem.
she walked back into {region} at {place} like a woman returning to a job. the road knows her here, and that cuts both ways.
depart: she left the canal at {place}, bound for {to}. the last of {region} she saw was a queue at a toll-house that had not moved in an hour.
out of {region} toward {to}. she took the old cut to avoid a checkpoint, lost a day, and considered it money well spent.
she turned off the glass at {place} and went for {to}. the ringing under her boots stopped, and the quiet took some getting used to.
leaving {region} for {to}. she paid a toll to get out, which she thought was the joke of the week. nobody else laughed.
she walked out of {region} toward {to} with her hood up. too many people here know her face now.
quiet_road: walked the glass canal toward {place}. nothing happened. she slept in the shade of it and dreamed of nothing.
{place}, and the light coming up off the canal-floor all day until her eyes ached. she did not close them. that is how people go missing.
three days on the canal road. the glass under her boots rang like a struck pot every mile or so, and she stopped flinching at it on the second day.
she found a shard of canal glass the size of her hand near {place} and carried it most of a day before she put it down.
the canal runs straight for eleven miles out of {place}. she walked all eleven and thought about nothing at all, and it was the best day she has had in a season.
{place}. someone had scratched a name into the glass. she read it, and walked on, and carried it the rest of the day.
the glass is cooler under the overhangs. she walked shade to shade all day out of {place}, like a lizard, and thought about how she must look.
the canal glass throws your own face back at you at the right hour. she has learned which hour, and walks the other way in it.
ambush_triumph: they came out of the canal shade at {place} and she put them down and stood there breathing and wanting more of it.
ambush_scrape_ally: they came at her and {ally} outside {place}, and the two of them put their backs to the canal wall and got through it. neither has said much since.
they came out of the sluice at {place}. she and {ally} put them back into it.
sickness: fever at {place}. she saw the canal running full and blue, and knew exactly what that meant, and walked toward it anyway.
beast_kill: she killed a canal-cat at {place} and sold the hide for less than it was worth, and knew it, and took the money.
something came out of the dry canal at {place}. she got it in the eye on the second pass. she is telling people it was the first.
relic: she dug something out of the canal bed at {place} that is the wrong weight for its size. she has not sold it. she has not shown it to anyone.
she found a piece of glass at {place} with something moving in it. it is not moving now. she has not sold it, and {power} takes an interest in things like that.
work: she scraped glass off a canal floor at {place} for eight days. {coin} coin, and blisters, and a wound elsewhere that healed while she was not watching.
rumor: someone at {place} said {power} was here before the canals. someone else said {power} is what happened to them.
an old man at {place} said the glass in the canals is not from the heat. he was laughed at. he did not seem to mind.

regions.name: the Sunder Flats
regions.blurb: Forty miles of salt with nothing on it. No cover, no water, no company.

arrive: she came out onto the flats at {place}. forty miles of nothing in every direction, and a man walking toward you takes a day to arrive.
into {region}. the salt throws the light back at her from underneath, and there is nowhere at all to stand out of it.
{region}, after {from}. no cover, no shade, no company. she counted her water twice the first morning and did not care for the answer.
she is on the flats now. {place} is four days out, and there is nothing between here and it but her own footprints going the wrong way.
she walked into {region} and the world went quiet and white. it is the emptiest country she knows, and she has come back to it on purpose.
depart: she came off the salt at {place} and went for {to}. she did not look back at {region}, which is a thing she has started noticing about herself.
out of {region} toward {to}. the first tree she saw, she stopped and put a hand on it, and would deny it.
leaving the flats for {to}. the salt is in her boots, her food, and every cut on her hands, and it will be weeks getting out.
she left {region} the way you leave a room you were not invited into. {to} by the end of the week.
she walked off the salt at {place} toward {to}, and her eyes hurt for three days after, and she says that is normal.
quiet_road: {place}, and then more flats. she rations her spit now, which she did not used to do, and has not decided how she feels about that.
quiet_camp_restless: she lay down while it was still light and listened to the flats tick as they cooled, and did not sleep, and did not much mind.
sickness: a fever, and the flats, and no shade past {place}. she got through it. she does not want to talk about how.
beast_kill: she brought down one of the big salt-hounds outside {place}. the meat was foul. the skin paid for a week.
relic: a thing from under the flats at {place}. it hums when the wind is from the east. she has begun walking with the wind at her back.
work: she hauled salt at {place} for {coin} coin. her back is ruined and everything else is better.
stranger: {name} came out of the flats near {place} with a blade and no story, and has been useful, and that is the whole of what she knows.
companion_talk: she and {ally} watched the light go off the flats and did not fill the silence.
rumor: a drunk at {place} said he had been down into the works below the flats. he was not lying. that is the part she keeps returning to.

regions.name: Wellmouth
regions.blurb: The only city with enough — and it has enough because nowhere else does.

arrive: she came into {region} at {place} and heard running water for the first time in a month, and it did something to her that she did not care for.
{region}: the only city in the country that has enough, and it has enough because everywhere else has not.
into {region} out of {from}. there is a fountain at the middle of it that runs day and night, and people bring their children to look at it.
she is in the city. {place}, and beds, and baths, and {agents} on every corner, and a queue at the water court four streets long.
she walked into {region} filthy and paid to stop being so, and sat in the water a long time, and did not think about what it cost.
depart: she left {region} for {to}. she filled everything she owns before she went, and the gate-clerk wrote down how much, and she watched him do it.
out of the city toward {to}. she was glad to go, and does not entirely believe herself about that.
she left {place} for {to}. the sound of the fountains followed her about a mile, and then it did not.
leaving {region} for {to}. she had been comfortable nine days and could feel it starting to soften her.
she walked out of {region} owing nobody anything, which she counts as an achievement, and toward {to}, which is not.
quiet_road: a day in the city and nothing in it. she walked the length of {place} twice, bought nothing, and was watched the whole time.
{place}. she stood an hour in a queue to look at water she did not need, and could not afterwards have told you why.
she slept indoors at {place}, behind a door that locked, and lay listening to the fountains, and did not trust the sound of them.
nothing happened at {place}. she spent the day among more people than she had seen in a month and spoke to none of them, and it was restful, and then it was not.
work: a week carrying water in {place} for {coin} coin — carrying it for people who already live where the water is. she thought about that all week.
{coin} coin for eight days on the door of a water-house at {place}, turning away people who looked a great deal like her.
she kept order in the queue at {place} for {coin} coin. she was good at it, and hated every hour of it.
rumor: in {place} they say the city draws its water from no well at all. they say it quietly, and then they talk about something else.
a clerk at {place} told her the water court has never once ruled against {power}. he laughed. then he stopped laughing.
heard at {place}: the fountains have run without stopping for a hundred years, and nobody will say what is underneath them.
charity: {coin} coin to the far end of the queue at {place}, where people had been standing three days. she did not wait to be thanked.
she paid the water-fee for a family at {place} — {coin} coin. the clerk stamped it without looking up, and that was that.
cache: {coin} coin behind a loose brick in a room she rented at {place}. the last tenant never came back for it. she asked after him. nobody would say.
she found {coin} coin sewn into a mattress at {place}, and slept on it two more nights before she took it.
power_moves: there were {agents} at both ends of her street in {place} for three days, and in three days they did nothing at all.
the water court at {place} sent for her. she did not go. nothing happened, and the nothing is the part that frightens her.

regions.name: Reed End
regions.blurb: The last green in the country, and it is only green because something in it eats what comes to drink.

arrive: she came into {region} at {place} — the last green in the country, and it is only green because something in it eats whatever comes down to drink.
{region}: reeds over her head, water at her boots, and things moving in it that she cannot see and has stopped pretending she can.
into the reeds out of {from}. it is the only place she has been in a year where she was cold at night, and she slept badly, and gratefully.
she is in {region} now. {place} is an hour in, and everything past it is water and noise and green light.
she walked into the wet country at {place}. there are birds here. she had forgotten about birds.
depart: she came out of {region} at {place} and went for {to} with her boots rotting off her feet.
out of the reeds toward {to}. she killed something on the way out and did not stop to sell it, which tells you what she thought of the reeds.
she left {region} for {to}. she has a fever coming, and she knows exactly where she got it.
leaving the green for {to}. she stood at the edge of it a while first, which is not like her.
she walked out of {region} toward {to} and did not stop until the ground was dry, and then went on a while more.
quiet_road: a day in the reeds out of {place}, water to her knees, and nothing to show for it but leeches.
{place}. something big moved in the water twenty feet from her and did not come out, and she did not go in.
she walked the boardwalks at {place} all day without once putting a foot on the ground, and slept in a hut on stilts, and slept well.
nothing happened at {place}. the birds went up all at once in the evening for no reason she could see, and she did not sleep afterwards.
beast_kill: she killed something in the shallows at {place} that had been taking dogs. it had teeth in a double row and she sold every one of them.
a long ugly kill in the water at {place}. it took an hour and she was waist-deep for all of it.
she put a spear through something at {place} that came up under the boat. the boatman would not take her money afterwards.
work: a week cutting reed at {place} for {coin} coin. her hands are in ribbons and she slept every night like the dead.
{coin} coin for a week guiding people through the wet country out of {place}. she brought all of them out, which is well above the usual rate.
sickness: the fever she took out of the water at {place} put her down for five days. she has had it before. it comes back.
she drank at {place} because she was out and had no choice, and paid for it for a fortnight.
rumor: at {place} they say the reeds were planted. not grown — planted. who by is where the story stops.
a fenman at {place} said the green only survives because {power} permits it, and that nothing is permitted for free.

regions.name: the Bone Reach
regions.blurb: Deep desert and the old works. Nothing lives here, which is why there is anything left to find.

arrive: she went out into {region}, where the old works are, and where people go once they have decided that coin is worth more to them than water.
{region}. nothing lives here. that is precisely why there is anything left in it to find.
into the reach out of {from}. the ruins start about a day in and they do not stop, and they are not the ruins of anything she can name.
she is in {region}. {place} was a city once. she is fairly sure it was not a city of people.
she came into the reach at {place} with two weeks of water and the arithmetic already worrying her.
depart: she came out of {region} at {place} and went for {to} carrying more than she went in with, which is not the same as being better off.
out of the reach toward {to}. she left something out there on purpose, and has not said what.
she left {region} for {to} with her water gone and eleven miles still to walk. she made it. she has been quiet about it since.
leaving the reach for {to}. she looked back once at the works, and they were exactly where she left them, and she cannot say why that unsettles her.
she walked out of {region} toward {to}. whatever the reach counts, it counted her, and she felt it do it.
quiet_road: four days into the reach out of {place} and she has seen nothing alive — not a bird, not a track. she is rationing hard and talking to nobody.
{place}. she walked through a doorway standing on its own in the open, with nothing on either side of it, and did not walk back through it.
nothing at {place} but the works, and the works do not change, and she has been looking at them for six days.
she built a fire at {place} out of something that was not wood, and did not ask herself what it was.
cache: {coin} coin in a chamber under {place} that had been sealed. she broke the seal. she thinks about that.
she found {coin} coin at {place} laid out in a row, evenly spaced, as though counted. she took it, and did not put anything in its place.
{coin} coin at {place}, in a room with no door, which she got into anyway, and would rather not explain how.
relic: she took something out of the works at {place} that is warm, and stays warm, and she has stopped putting a hand on it to check.
a thing from under {place} that she cannot make herself throw away. she has tried twice.
she found something at {place} that is a tool. she is sure of that much. she is not sure it was made for a hand.
rumor: out at {place} they say the works were neither built nor dug, and the man who told her had been out here eleven years.
heard at {place} that {power} does not come into the reach. she has decided that is a comfort. she keeps having to decide it.
power_moves: there were {agents} out in the reach at {place}, days from any well, standing where nobody stands. they watched her go past.
she found a marker at {place} that had not been there a week before, with her name on it and no other word.

quiet_road: passed through {place}. she paid for a cup and drank it too fast and was angry with herself for an hour.
a long dry stretch past {place}. she talked to the mule. the mule died last spring. she talked anyway.
nothing at {place} but a dead well and a man selling the story of how it died. she did not buy it.
she walked at night out of {place} and slept through the white part of the day, and woke with the taste of iron, and does not know why.
work of a kind at {place}: holding a horse for a man who did not tip. she has been paid worse for worse.
the wind came up past {place} and took the top inch off the road. she walked into it for six hours. nothing else happened.
{place}. a boy asked her where she was going and she told him the truth and he lost interest immediately, which was fair.
two days along the shattered stretch out of {place}. she counted seven cairns and did not stop at any of them.
no water on the road out of {place} but what she brought. she brought enough. she is quietly proud of that and would not say so.
a caravan passed her going the other way past {place}. nobody spoke. everyone looked at everyone's flasks, and everyone pretended not to.
{place}, and a day so hot the flies gave up. she kept walking, which is worse thinking than the flies managed.
she washed her feet in a cup of water at {place} and felt like a criminal, and did it anyway.
a slow week toward {place}. she has begun to sing, badly, when the road is empty. she stops when it is not.
dust and light and the road, and at the end of it {place}, and nothing waiting there either.
she earned a few coin at {place} carrying water for a man who could afford not to carry it. she thought about that all evening.
a dry crossing to {place}. she chewed a strap for the wet of it — a habit she picked up somewhere and cannot remember where.
she slept in the lee of a broken sluice near {place}. the stone gave its heat back all night. she slept badly, and warm.
nothing happened on the road out of {place}, and it took nine days to happen.
she is walking the long way around {place}. she has told herself three separate reasons and does not believe any of them.
hot wind, hard light, {place} somewhere ahead of her. she has stopped checking how far.
she gave a stranger directions outside {place} and got them slightly wrong on purpose and has not worked out why.
a day of nothing at {place}. she cleaned her kit twice. the second time was not about the kit.
she watched a cart of {agents} go by outside {place} and stood very still until it had gone, which was not necessary, and she knows it.
she took the low road past {place} to save half a day and it cost her a full one. she has done this before.
{place}, and a man there who remembered her, and she could not place him, and she talked her way through it.
bought bread at {place}. it was bad bread. she ate all of it.
she reached {place} with water to spare, and sat down, and did not know what to do with the extra hour.
a quiet stretch past {place}. she mended her boots badly and walked on them and they held, and she is treating that as a verdict on something.
nothing on the road to {place} but heat-shake and the smell of hot stone. she has stopped seeing shapes in the shake. that took years.
she passed a dry cistern outside {place} with the ladder still in it and did not go down, and spent the night deciding whether that was sense or cowardice.
she walked past the bones of something big near {place}, picked clean and bleached. she did not slow down.
{place}. she paid a woman for a bed and slept on the floor beside it out of habit, and could not have explained that to anyone.
a day of walking to {place} and a day of waiting in it, and the waiting was harder.
she has walked the stretch past {place} enough to know where the shade falls at every hour. she is not sure whether that is competence or a sentence.
she spoke to nobody for four days out of {place}. she noticed on the fourth. that is longer than it used to take.
a good road, flat and hard, out of {place}. she made twenty miles and felt almost young, and paid for it in the morning.
she stopped at the shrine-stone outside {place} because everyone stops. she left nothing and took nothing and felt watched anyway.
a long day on the road to {place} and nothing in it but the walking. she has had worse days, and fewer of them lately.
{place}. she sat in the shade of a wall for an hour and watched people come and go and did not speak to any of them.
she made {place} by dusk with her water half gone and her feet in worse shape than that.
nothing happened on the way to {place}. she has begun to notice the days when nothing happens, and to count them.
she walked behind a herd of goats for six miles out of {place} and could not get past them, and stopped minding around the third.
{place}, and a hot wind out of the east all day, and grit in everything she owns.
she came into {place} with a stone in her boot she had been ignoring for two days. she took it out. it was nothing at all.
a slow road to {place}. she has begun talking to herself out loud, and has decided to call it efficiency.
she reached {place} a day earlier than she meant to, and spent the day doing nothing, badly.
quiet_camp_healed: made camp while the sun was still up and let a wound have the air. it is closing. she checked it twice, which is once more than necessary.
she stopped early. the wound in her side has stopped weeping. she poured a little water over it and resented every drop.
an early camp, and a wound that has decided to close on its own. she did nothing to earn it and is taking the credit anyway.
she dressed the cut on her arm properly for once, with clean cloth, and it is already better, and she is annoyed by the lesson in that.
camp before dusk. she picked the scab, looked at it, and put the bandage back on, and knows exactly how stupid that was.
she rested. it worked. she does not like how much she resents that.
the wound is closing. she has stopped bracing when she rolls over at night, and only noticed because she slept.
she sat with her boots off and her back against a warm stone and let the day end without her. one wound less.
an early fire, a wound cleaned, and a whole hour of nothing. she filled it with worrying and healed regardless.
she made camp early and slept nine hours and woke furious with herself, and better.
she cut the dead skin away and it bled clean, which is what you want, and she said so out loud to nobody.
she stopped walking at noon because the wound told her to. she is learning to listen to it, slowly, and only when it shouts.
camp early. the good cloth is gone so she used the bad, and it is closing anyway. bodies do not know the difference. she does.
the swelling is down. she can make a fist. she made a fist several times, unnecessarily.
she washed a wound with water she could have drunk. she has done the arithmetic and it still comes out right, and it still hurt.
she stopped early, for once, and the wound closed, for once, and she is dimly aware that these two facts are related.
an early camp, and she did nothing all evening but let herself mend, and she was bored, and boredom is a luxury she has not had in months.
quiet_camp_restless: she made camp early with nothing to tend and no reason to stop, and stopped anyway, and lay awake.
nothing hurts. she cleaned a knife that was already clean.
an early camp and a whole body that works. she does not know what to do with the evening. she never has.
she is not wounded, not hungry, not hunted. she sat up until the fire went out, looking for the catch.
camp before dark, nothing to mend, nothing to dress. she counted her coin twice, got the same number, and was disappointed.
she stopped early and could not settle. she walked the perimeter three times. there was no perimeter.
no wounds. she sharpened everything she owns.
a whole night with nothing in it. she has been in worse nights and slept better.
she stopped for no reason and could not name one, and it bothered her more than a wound would have.
nothing to do. she reorganised the pack, which is now organised exactly as it was.
she is fine. that is the entire report, and she turned it over half the night.
an early camp, a good fire, and nothing to think about but herself, which is why she does not make early camps.
she stopped early and her hands wanted work. she gave them rope, and took it apart, and gave it to them again.
no pain anywhere. she keeps checking, the way you check a pocket.
camp early. she ate well and slept badly, which is the usual arrangement, in reverse.
she built a good fire and then sat away from it, out in the cold, and could not have told you why.
she had two hours of daylight left and stopped anyway, and spent both of them irritated about it.
nothing needed doing, so she found something and did it slowly on purpose, to make it last.
she is uninjured and unhurried and she hates it, and would deny hating it, and did, out loud, to the fire.
a still night. she heard her own heart and took it for footsteps for a moment, and was almost relieved.
she made camp early and did not sleep and did not rest, and got up at dawn with nothing to show for the night but the night.
she lay awake listening to the ground give back its heat, and counted nothing in particular, and did not sleep.
ambush_triumph: four of them at {place}, and she was better than all four, and knew it by the second one. she is not proud of how good that felt.
ambushed at {place}. she walked away with their purses, their water, and a grin she could not get off her face for a day.
they picked her badly at {place}. she has told the story twice already and improved it both times.
she killed two men at {place} and took a third's boots while he watched. she has thought about his face since, and not with regret.
an ambush at {place} that lasted about as long as it takes to draw. she has been waiting years for one to go like that.
three of them tried her outside {place} and it was finished before the third had his knife up. she has been in a good mood since, and knows what that says about her.
ambush_ruin_ally: jumped at {place}, and it went badly, and {ally} stepped into a blade that had her name on it. she is carrying that, and the wound, and a good deal less coin.
they had her down at {place} before she understood it was happening. {ally} took a knife in the shoulder pulling them off her. she has not said thank you and does not know how.

it went wrong at {place}. she lost blood and coin and every illusion about how good she is. {ally} took the cut that would have finished her, and has not mentioned it once, which is worse than mentioning it.
ambush_ruin: they took her at {place} and left her with nothing but the walk to the next well. she was a long time getting up.
it went badly at {place}. she does not remember the end of it, and her coin is gone, and she has resolved to be careful for a while.
four men at {place}, and she was wrong about every one of them. she crawled out of it. she says she will not make that mistake twice.
ambushed at {place} and beaten, properly beaten, for the first time in years. she has been quiet since, and slower, and she is checking corners again.
ambush_scrape_ally: ambushed at {place}. {ally} took the two on the left without being asked. that is the whole of it, and it is not nothing.
a bad hour at {place}. she and {ally} came out of it upright and split what the men had been carrying, and did not count it carefully.
jumped at {place}. she heard {ally} laugh in the middle of it, which she found appalling, and thinks about often.
she and {ally} were ambushed near {place} and fought like people who have done it together before. they have not. they will again.
men in the road at {place}. she went left, {ally} went right, and neither of them had to say so.
it was close at {place}. {ally} was closer than she was, and stayed.
ambushed at {place}. afterwards {ally} sat down and shook for a while, and she let them, and said nothing, and stood watch.
a scrap at {place} that could have gone the other way. {ally} bound her arm afterwards without being asked, and did it well.
she took a cut at {place} keeping it off {ally} and has not mentioned it, and {ally} noticed anyway.
she and {ally} were ambushed at {place}. she has stopped counting how many times now, and so, she suspects, has {ally}.
bandits at {place}, and a short ugly fight, and afterwards {ally} handed her a flask without a word and she drank from it without wiping it.
jumped near {place}. {ally} is faster than she is. she has decided this is useful and not to think about it further.
it went badly at {place} for about ten seconds. {ally} made it go the other way.
they came at her and {ally} on the road out of {place}, and the two of them put their backs together and got through it. neither has said much since.
men in the road at {place}. she and {ally} put them down, and afterwards sat in the dirt getting their breath back, side by side, saying nothing.
ambush_scrape: ambushed alone at {place}. she won it, barely, and afterwards sat down in the road for longer than she would admit.
two men at {place}. she came away with a cut, their coin, and no particular feeling about it.
it was close at {place}, and there was nobody to be close with, and she noticed.
jumped near {place}. she got through it. she has a wound and forty fewer illusions.
she was alone at {place} when they came. she is still alone. that is the report.
a fight at {place} she should not have won, and did, and she is not asking why too loudly.
sickness: fever took her outside {place} and she walked through it, because stopping costs water. that was stupid, and she knew it while she did it.
she was sick for three days on the road out of {place} and does not remember two of them.
a fever at {place}, and she kept walking. she has always believed you can outpace a thing like that, and has never once been right.
something in the water at {place}. she was down a day and up too early, and paid for it for a week.
she went hot and grey past {place} and told a stranger she was fine, and the stranger did not believe her, and she resented that.
sick on the road out of {place}. she drank her ration hot and it did not help and she drank it anyway.
she brought up water she could not spare outside {place}, and knelt in the road a while afterwards doing the arithmetic.
she has been ill since {place}. she is walking it off, which is not a thing that works, which she knows.
a bad night at {place}, shaking under everything she owns. she was better by morning and worse by noon.
sick, and alone, and moving. she has done this before. it does not get easier. it gets familiar.
she caught something at {place} and gave it three days, and it gave her a week.
she woke at {place} unable to stand, and stood.
a fever past {place}, and a long argument with herself about whether to stop, which she won, and lost.
she was ill outside {place} and hid it from everyone who passed. there was no reason to. she did it anyway.
she has been coughing since {place} and telling people it is the dust. it may be the dust.
